concerns.text: Task T014 requires implementing 'Schaefer‑100 parcellation to DWI -> Binary Adjacency' but does not specify the input file format (e.g., .trk, .tck, .nii.gz) or the specific atlas file path. The implementer cannot execute this without knowing which raw data files to read and which atlas definition to load.

Task T015 requires computing 'Global Efficiency (on weighted graph)' but does not specify which weighted graph to use (the one from T014 or a separate unthresholded one). The plan distinguishes between binary (thresholded) and weighted (unthresholded) graphs, but the task description is ambiguous, risking implementation of the wrong graph type.

Task T022 requires enumerating 'all 3‑node subgraphs' but does not specify the input graph type (binary or weighted) or the source file path. The plan specifies motifs are calculated on the 'binary' graph, but the task description lacks this critical constraint, making the deliverable non-deterministic.

Task T026 requires computing z-scores at 'three density thresholds (10%, 20%, 30%)' but does not specify the input graph source. It is unclear if these thresholds apply to the binary graph from T014 or if new binary graphs must be generated from the weighted graph. The task assumes context not present in the description.

Task T031 requires computing 'partial Pearson/Spearman correlations (controlling for network density)' but does not specify the input data sources (which JSON/CSV files contain the motif z-scores, rsFC strength, and density values). The task names the algorithm but not the data artifacts to read.

Task T036 requires generating a PDF with 'scatter plots, CIs, p-values, and permutation results per motif' but does not specify the input data file path (e.g., `results/correlation_results.json`). The implementer cannot execute this without knowing where the statistical results are stored.

Task T001 'Create project structure per implementation plan' is too coarse. It covers the entire directory setup for the project. The atomizer will split this, but it should be flagged as 'writing' to indicate it needs splitting into specific directory creation tasks (e.g., 'Create code/, tests/, data/raw/ directories').

Task T040 'Code cleanup and refactoring' is too coarse and vague. It does not name a specific artifact or criteria for 'cleanup'. This is a classic 'writing' class issue that the atomizer will need to split into specific refactoring tasks or remove if not actionable.

Task T041 'Performance optimization across all stories (verify SC-002 compliance)' is too coarse. It bundles optimization for all stories and relies on a success criteria check. It should be split into specific optimization tasks for the bottleneck modules (e.g., 'Optimize motifs.py for SC-002').
